source_alignment.subject: Mathematics
source_alignment.source_statement: Simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions to maintain equivalence by collecting like terms.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This Year 7 bridge pack focuses on recognising like terms and preserving signs.

objective.child_goal: I can collect like terms without changing the expression's value.
objective.statement: Simplify algebraic expressions by collecting like terms.
misconceptions: Combines unlike terms such as x and x squared.
Loses negative signs when collecting terms.
Treats the coefficient and variable as separate addable parts.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A part of an expression, such as 3x or -2.
vocabulary.audio_script: A term is one part of an algebra expression.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A labelled algebra tile or token.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Terms with the same variable part, such as 3x and 5x.
vocabulary.audio_script: Like terms have matching variable parts.

vocabulary.visual_anchor: The number label attached to a variable tile.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Sort numbers, x terms and x squared terms into different trays.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Activate the idea that term families must match before they can combine.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Terms can only join if their variable part matches.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three lab trays labelled number, x and x squared.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: The lab sorter only combines tokens from the same family.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce like terms as same-family algebra objects.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Like terms are from the same family. x terms join x terms.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Tokens snap only into matching trays.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: 3x + 5x - 2 becomes 8x - 2. The x terms join; the number stays separate.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model collection while preserving unlike number terms.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Three x plus five x is eight x. The minus two is a number term, so it stays separate.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: 3 x tiles join 5 x tiles; the -2 token remains in the number tray.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag each term with its sign into the right tray.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Make the sign travel with the term.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The sign belongs to the term. Move them together.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Negative signs are attached to tiles as tabs.

teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Detect whether the learner combines unlike terms.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Check the variable part before you combine.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: x and x squared tiles reject a merge attempt.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach the lab assistant why 2x + 3 cannot become 5x.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to confirm the boundary between variables and constants.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Can you explain why a number term cannot join an x term?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The assistant tries a wrong merge and waits for correction.

manipulatives.purpose: Represent algebra terms as sortable tokens so learners can collect only like terms.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners drag signed terms into family trays, combine coefficients and rebuild the expression.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard selection cycles through terms; reduced-motion mode uses colour and outline changes instead of snapping animation.

manipulatives.purpose: Make equivalence feel like a construction challenge where every legal move must preserve the expression's value.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners choose combine, expand, reorder or undo moves, test expressions at sample values and repair one deliberately invalid transformation.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: All moves are available through a labelled keyboard menu; coefficients are spoken with signs, colour is redundant and a static move history supports working memory.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave negative terms only after learners preserve signs in simpler positive-only expressions.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build recognition of like and unlike terms before symbolic simplification.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: signed term set + family trays + reject invalid merge checks
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include constants, x terms, y terms and squared terms only as learners are ready.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Practise simplifying expressions where signs must travel with terms.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: expression + token bank + ordered answer slots + sign tab animation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review should check all equivalent answer forms accepted by the marker.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Require learners to explain why terms can or cannot be collected.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: expression pair + explanation stem + expected keyword set
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Short-response marker must accept age-appropriate wording, not only exact model answers.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use distractors that map to sign, unlike-term and coefficient misconceptions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: target expression + correct simplification + three misconception distractors
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Every distractor should have a named repair route rather than generic wrong feedback.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Feed spaced review and later algebra transfer with varied expressions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review scheduler selects positive, negative and unlike-term cases by weakness
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep retrieval focused on simplification until substitution is introduced as a later objective.

body.prompt: Sort 4x, -2, 3x and 5 into the x-term and number-term trays.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

hints: Terms with x belong together.
Keep each sign attached to its number.
question_variants.explanation: 4x and 3x are like x terms. -2 and 5 are number terms, so they belong in the number tray.

feedback.correct: Correct. 4x and 3x are like x terms. -2 and 5 are number terms, so they belong in the number tray.
feedback.retry: Try again calmly: Terms with x belong together. Keep each sign attached to its number. No progress is lost for checking or changing your answer.
feedback.repair: Repair the unlike terms combined route: Terms with x belong together. Then use the evidence to explain your choice.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the unlike terms combined idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

body.prompt: Simplify 3x + 5x - 2.

hints: Only the x terms combine.
The -2 is a number term and stays separate.
question_variants.explanation: 3x and 5x are like terms, so they combine to make 8x. The -2 remains separate.

body.prompt: Simplify 7x - 2x + 4.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

hints: Move the minus sign with 2x.
7x - 2x is 5x.
question_variants.explanation: 7x minus 2x leaves 5x, and the +4 remains because it is a number term.

feedback.correct: Correct. 7x minus 2x leaves 5x, and the +4 remains because it is a number term.
feedback.retry: Try again calmly: Move the minus sign with 2x. 7x - 2x is 5x. No progress is lost for checking or changing your answer.
feedback.repair: Repair the lost negative sign route: Move the minus sign with 2x. Then use the evidence to explain your choice.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the lost negative sign idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

body.prompt: Explain why 4x + 3x^2 cannot be simplified to 7x^2.
body.supported_interaction: Use labelled touch or keyboard controls. Equivalent routes include single-switch scanning, eye-gaze dwell selection, AAC, pointing, partner or adult-supported response; fine dragging, handwriting and speech are never required.

expected_answer.value: They are unlike terms because x and x squared are different variable parts.

hints: Compare the variable parts.
x and x squared belong to different families.
question_variants.explanation: 4x and 3x^2 are unlike terms. Their variable parts are different, so they cannot be collected together.

feedback.correct: Correct. 4x and 3x^2 are unlike terms. Their variable parts are different, so they cannot be collected together.
feedback.retry: Try again calmly: Compare the variable parts. x and x squared belong to different families. No progress is lost for checking or changing your answer.
feedback.repair: Repair the unlike terms combined route: Compare the variable parts. Then use the evidence to explain your choice.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the unlike terms combined idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

body.prompt: Algebra-tile lab 1: sort each signed term by its complete variable part.
body.representation: labelled positive and negative algebra tiles with a text inventory
body.concrete_scaffold: patterned family trays with detachable signed coefficient counters and text labels
body.visual_scaffold: variable families use labels, shapes and patterns as well as colour; signs remain physically and textually attached to terms
body.symbolic_scaffold: original expression remains visible beside a signed term inventory and a one-move-at-a-time history
body.manipulative_alternative: same task with large-print magnetic term cards and signed coefficient counters
body.static_alternative: numbered text list, family table or labelled area model containing all information without animation
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant state changes and persistent outlines with no snapping, bouncing, countdown or flashing
sentence_stems: This move preserves the value because ___.

hints: Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family.
Constants have no variable part; x and x^2 are different families.
question_variants.explanation: The families are determined by the complete variable part, not by coefficient size or sign. x terms: 2x, -2x; constant terms: -1, 1. Sorting first protects equivalence before any coefficients are collected.

feedback.correct: Puzzle route verified. The families are determined by the complete variable part, not by coefficient size or sign. x terms: 2x, -2x; constant terms: -1, 1. Sorting first protects equivalence before any coefficients are collected.
feedback.try_again: No speed score and no lost progress. Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family.
feedback.misconception: Test the 'unlike terms combined' route against the signed model, then use the second hint.
feedback.strategy: Use the text-only tray list or place one signed counter at a time; say the variable part before choosing a tray.
feedback.equivalence_check: A legal simplification or expansion must keep the same output for every allowed value; a counterexample disproves equivalence.
feedback.retry: Try again calmly: Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family. Constants have no variable part; x and x^2 are different families. No progress is lost for checking or changing your answer.
feedback.repair: Repair the unlike terms combined route: Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family. Then use the evidence to explain your choice.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the unlike terms combined idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

gamification.objective: Unlock one algebra bridge node by justifying a value-preserving move.
gamification.strategic_choice: Choose tiles, family trays, a value table or symbols; route choice is not scored.

body.concrete_scaffold: patterned family trays with detachable signed coefficient counters and text labels
body.visual_scaffold: variable families use labels, shapes and patterns as well as colour; signs remain physically and textually attached to terms
body.symbolic_scaffold: original expression remains visible beside a signed term inventory and a one-move-at-a-time history
body.manipulative_alternative: same task with large-print magnetic term cards and signed coefficient counters
body.static_alternative: numbered text list, family table or labelled area model containing all information without animation
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant state changes and persistent outlines with no snapping, bouncing, countdown or flashing
sentence_stems: This move preserves the value because ___.

hints: Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family.
Constants have no variable part; x and x^2 are different families.
question_variants.explanation: The families are determined by the complete variable part, not by coefficient size or sign. x terms: 3x, -2x; constant terms: -1, 3. Sorting first protects equivalence before any coefficients are collected.

feedback.correct: Puzzle route verified. The families are determined by the complete variable part, not by coefficient size or sign. x terms: 3x, -2x; constant terms: -1, 3. Sorting first protects equivalence before any coefficients are collected.
feedback.try_again: No speed score and no lost progress. Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family.
feedback.misconception: Test the 'unlike terms combined' route against the signed model, then use the second hint.
feedback.strategy: Use the text-only tray list or place one signed counter at a time; say the variable part before choosing a tray.
feedback.equivalence_check: A legal simplification or expansion must keep the same output for every allowed value; a counterexample disproves equivalence.
feedback.retry: Try again calmly: Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family. Constants have no variable part; x and x^2 are different families. No progress is lost for checking or changing your answer.
feedback.repair: Repair the unlike terms combined route: Keep the sign attached and ignore coefficient size when choosing a family. Then use the evidence to explain your choice.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the unlike terms combined idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

gamification.objective: Unlock one algebra bridge node by justifying a value-preserving move.
gamification.strategic_choice: Choose tiles, family trays, a value table or symbols; route choice is not scored.

Addition signs make all terms like.

The x terms share variable part x; the constant 2 belongs to a different family.
The terms beside plus signs all become 10x.
body.explanation_stem: These terms can or cannot be combined because ___
body.accepted_response: equivalent age-appropriate wording using the variable-part relationship
body.representation: large-print numbered cards with no movement required
body.concrete_scaffold: signed counters placed in patterned variable-family trays
body.visual_scaffold: variable families use labels, shapes and patterns as well as colour; signs remain physically and textually attached to terms
body.symbolic_scaffold: original expression remains visible beside a signed term inventory and a one-move-at-a-time history
body.manipulative_alternative: same task with large-print magnetic term cards and signed coefficient counters
body.static_alternative: numbered text list, family table or labelled area model containing all information without animation
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant state changes and persistent outlines with no snapping, bouncing, countdown or flashing
sentence_stems: This move preserves the value because ___.

expected_answer.value: The x terms share variable part x; the constant 2 belongs to a different family.

hints: Compare the complete variable part of each term, including every variable and power.
Then describe what the coefficient counts and keep each sign attached.
question_variants.explanation: Reordering can place like terms together because addition is commutative, but it cannot change a constant into an x term. The equivalent simplification is 8x + 2.

feedback.correct: Puzzle route verified. Reordering can place like terms together because addition is commutative, but it cannot change a constant into an x term. The equivalent simplification is 8x + 2.
feedback.try_again: No speed score and no lost progress. Compare the complete variable part of each term, including every variable and power.
feedback.misconception: Test the 'constant combined with variable' route against the signed model, then use the second hint.
feedback.strategy: Say or select the variable part first, then complete the scaffold: they are like/unlike because ___. Exact model wording is not required.
feedback.equivalence_check: A legal simplification or expansion must keep the same output for every allowed value; a counterexample disproves equivalence.
feedback.retry: Try again calmly: Compare the complete variable part of each term, including every variable and power. Then describe what the coefficient counts and keep each sign attached. No progress is lost for checking or changing your answer.
feedback.repair: Repair the constant combined with variable route: Compare the complete variable part of each term, including every variable and power. Then use the evidence to explain your choice.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the constant combined with variable idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

body.prompt: Term-family trays: Simplify 5x - (2x + 3).
body.representation: patterned family trays and signed coefficient counters
body.concrete_scaffold: partitioned area model with every product region labelled
body.visual_scaffold: variable families use labels, shapes and patterns as well as colour; signs remain physically and textually attached to terms

body.static_alternative: numbered text list, family table or labelled area model containing all information without animation
body.reduced_motion_alternative: instant state changes and persistent outlines with no snapping, bouncing, countdown or flashing
sentence_stems: This move preserves the value because ___.

hints: Name each operation and keep signs attached before calculating.
Check the choice with a concrete model, structure or one carefully substituted value.
question_variants.explanation: Subtracting the bracket subtracts both terms: 5x - 2x - 3. Collecting the x terms gives 3x, and the constant remains -3.

feedback.correct: Puzzle route verified. Subtracting the bracket subtracts both terms: 5x - 2x - 3. Collecting the x terms gives 3x, and the constant remains -3.
feedback.try_again: No speed score and no lost progress. Name each operation and keep signs attached before calculating.
feedback.misconception: Test the 'minus not distributed' route against the signed model, then use the second hint.
feedback.strategy: Rebuild the step using partitioned area model with every product region labelled, then compare the misconception choice with the invariant meaning of the expression.
feedback.equivalence_check: A legal simplification or expansion must keep the same output for every allowed value; a counterexample disproves equivalence.
feedback.retry: Try again calmly: Name each operation and keep signs attached before calculating. Check the choice with a concrete model, structure or one carefully substituted value. No progress is lost for checking or changing your answer.
feedback.repair: Repair the minus not distributed route: Name each operation and keep signs attached before calculating. Then use the evidence to explain your choice.
feedback.misconception_check: Check the minus not distributed idea against the task evidence.
feedback.support_message: Touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing and adult-supported routes are valid; speed, speech and fine-motor precision are not scored.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Combines unlike terms such as x and x squared.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects an answer that adds coefficients across different variable powers.
repair_sequence: Sort terms into x, x squared and number trays.
Try to merge unlike trays and show the mismatch.
Combine only the matching tray and rebuild the expression.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner rejects an unlike-term merge and simplifies a matched-term expression.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Loses negative signs when collecting terms.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner gives a coefficient that treats subtraction as addition.
repair_sequence: Attach each sign to its term.
Combine positive and negative coefficients on a balance strip.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner simplifies an expression with one negative x term correctly.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Treats the coefficient and variable as separate addable parts.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner changes 3x into 3 + x or combines constants into variable terms.
repair_sequence: Show coefficient as a count of matching variable tiles.
Ask learner to rebuild 4x from four x tiles.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner describes coefficient as number of variable groups.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use clean trays, no flashing error states and a quiet success outline.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace snap and bounce animations with instant tray placement and static highlights.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read expressions aloud using clear term pauses and sign language.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Chunk expressions into draggable signed terms and avoid dense paragraph prompts.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Break the task into sort, combine and rebuild stages with one visible goal at a time.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow term sorting without timers and reveal one expression at a time.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Frame wrong merges as lab tests that reveal term families.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach term, coefficient, variable and expression with visual examples.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires accurate sorting and simplification across positive and single-negative expressions; secure requires explaining unlike terms and retaining sign rules over time.
evidence.parent_summary: Can collect matching algebra terms and explain why some terms cannot be combined.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Sorts like terms, preserves signs, simplifies expressions and explains the difference between variables, constants and powers.
next_actions: Add expressions with more than two like-term families.
Interleave substitution after term collection is secure.
Route to sign-preservation repair when subtraction errors recur.

qa.notes: Year 7 simplifying expressions reaches the 220-item pilot target with four preserved curated variants and 216 deterministic review candidates. The depth bank covers variable, term and coefficient meaning; collecting like terms; substitution; forming expressions; equivalence; distributive reasoning; and misconception diagnosis. Every generated item offers concrete and symbolic scaffolds, static text alternatives, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, oral and partner-mediated routes, rich strategy feedback, and private puzzle-mission progress without timers, streak loss, lives, leaderboards or peer comparison. Independent mathematics, teacher, SEND, accessibility, safeguarding, renderer and pilot review remain required before promotion.

accessibility_policy.no_timer_or_penalty: Learners are never timed for mastery and never lose earned progress for mistakes; incorrect attempts route to calm repair, replay and retry.
accessibility_policy.equivalent_response_routes: Every child-facing task must preserve an equivalent route through touch, keyboard, switch, eye-gaze, AAC, pointing, partner-mediated or adult-scribed response where appropriate.
accessibility_policy.audio_equivalent: Audio and read-aloud support must complement visible text, visuals and replay. Child-facing narration must use produced, human-listening-approved assets before release; browser TTS is not a teaching or phonics fallback.
accessibility_policy.reduced_motion: Reduced-motion and static alternatives must preserve the same curriculum evidence, feedback and reward meaning without relying on animation.
